elements.title: a monument to Pope John Paul II
elements.contents: ... that a monument to Pope John Paul II (pictured) was cast using more than seven million donated keys?

elements.title: a radio station
elements.contents: ... that the United States Armed Forces funded a radio station on Ascension Island that transmitted from an extinct volcano?

elements.title: John Laugharne
elements.contents: ... that John Laugharne suddenly died the night after his re-election in 1715?

elements.title: The Fantod Pack
elements.contents: ... that The Fantod Pack, a set of divination cards, predicts only misfortune for its users, including "unstable furniture", "misplaced confidence", and "worms"?

elements.title: John Naish
elements.contents: ... that author and sugarcane cutter John Naish was critical of the sugar industry's role in the oppression of Indigenous Australians?

elements.title: were found by a construction worker
elements.contents: ... that 2,920 gold coins, a gold chain from the Renaissance period, three gold medals, and an engraved copper casket were found by a construction worker?

elements.title: a Missouri TV station
elements.contents: ... that less than a year after the owner of a Missouri TV station declared that "cancellation of a network affiliation is almost unheard of", his station's affiliation was canceled?

elements.title: Bastardstown
elements.contents: ... that one journalist said that the only reason to go to Bastardstown is to say that you have been there?

elements.title: Laurentius Say
elements.contents: ... that a Golkar official claimed that he "would run around town naked" if Laurentius Say survived two terms as regent of Sikka?
